subject: every collector's VALUE_SCHEMA dict — schema enforcement
principal: Each collector declares a `VALUE_SCHEMA = {"type": "object", "required": [...]}` dict, but no code actually validates emitted data points against this schema. The dicts are documentation that pretends to be enforcement.
interest: A collector can emit data points whose `value` shape silently drifts from its declared schema. Downstream signals fitting on the dataset will see whatever shape the collector last produced, not the shape the schema claims. Drift is silent and only caught by ad-hoc inspection.
payoff: Replace each VALUE_SCHEMA dict with a Pydantic v2 model (https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/) that exports JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 (https://json-schema.org/specification.html). lib/data_point.py's validate() function calls the model on every data point. verify.py self-check fails if any data point violates its declared model.
re_trigger: any collector ships a data point whose value shape diverges from VALUE_SCHEMA and the divergence isn't caught by verify (i.e., the silent drift this debt warns about actually happens)
